2025 Season — Round 8

Monaco Grand Prix

Monaco — 78 laps — 20 drivers

Total lap inversions
1125
start→finish: 20
Volatility score
0.7212
inversions / (grid × laps)
Winner
NOR
Lando Norris
Biggest mover
BEA
+8 positions (Oliver Bearman)
Most overtaken
TSU
-5 positions (Yuki Tsunoda)
DNFs
2
retirements
On-track overtakes
4
of 90 total moves
Interventions
4 VSC
laps under neutralisation
Race Summary

The 2025 Monaco GP produced 1125 lap inversions across 78 laps, with a volatility score of 0.7212. Oliver Bearman (BEA) was the biggest mover, gaining 8 positions. There were 2 retirements which influenced race positions.

Inversions Per Lap
Each point shows how many active inversions existed that lap relative to the starting grid order. Higher peaks indicate more overtaking activity.
Cumulative Inversion Curve
Running total of inversions across the race. Steep sections indicate high-action phases. Flat sections mean positions locked in for multiple laps.
Lap-by-Lap Position Tracker
Each line is one driver's position through the race. P1 is at the top. Lines end where a driver retired. Use filters to isolate specific battles or storylines.
Quick select:18 / 20 drivers shown
Driver Position Changes
Positions gained (green) or lost (red) from grid start to final classification. DNF drivers shown in grey.
Position Heatmap
Rows = laps, columns = drivers sorted by grid position. Green = P1, red = last. More colour transitions = more movement = more chaos. Faded cells = DNF.

Race Chaos Timeline
Lap-by-lap breakdown of overtaking activity by type. Green = genuine on-track passes, orange = pit-cycle position changes, yellow = moves under Safety Car / VSC, red = DNF-induced gains. Yellow dashed lines mark neutralisation laps.
Overtake Classification
Breaking down how position changes occurred during the race. Only data from re-ingested races (fetch_race_data.py v2) will show full classification.
90
Total position gains
On-track
4 (4%)
Pit cycle
73 (81%)
SC / VSC
0 (0%)
DNF-induced
13 (14%)
Adjusted Volatility
Three inversion curves for comparison. Raw: unfiltered (dashed grey). Pit-adjusted: removes drivers on in/out-laps so pit stops don't inflate inversions (green). SC-neutralized: SC and VSC laps weighted at 25% to reduce artificial compression effects (yellow).
Raw
0.7212
Pit-adjusted
0.6212
SC-neutralized
0.7
Driver Battle Index
The most intense wheel-to-wheel fights of the race, ranked by a combined intensity score (position swaps + laps within 1 position). Higher = more sustained wheel-to-wheel racing.
LECvsNOR
NOR wins
2 position swaps74 laps within 1 posIntensity: 39
LECvsPIA
LEC wins
0 position swaps75 laps within 1 posIntensity: 37.5
LAWvsOCO
OCO wins
2 position swaps63 laps within 1 posIntensity: 33.5
HADvsOCO
HAD wins
4 position swaps58 laps within 1 posIntensity: 33
ALBvsSAITEAMMATE
ALB wins
2 position swaps59 laps within 1 posIntensity: 31.5
ANTvsTSU
TSU wins
1 position swap58 laps within 1 posIntensity: 30
ALBvsLAW
LAW wins
2 position swaps55 laps within 1 posIntensity: 29.5
RUSvsSAI
SAI wins
0 position swaps59 laps within 1 posIntensity: 29.5
BORvsCOL
COL wins
2 position swaps53 laps within 1 posIntensity: 28.5
ANTvsRUSTEAMMATE
RUS wins
0 position swaps54 laps within 1 posIntensity: 27
Tyre Strategy Timeline
Lap-by-lap view of each driver's tyre compounds through the race. Solid = new tyres, faded with border = used tyres. Hover over any stint bar to see compound, stint length, pit lap, duration, and net position change.
Quick select:20 / 20 drivers shown

Medium

Lap
What If No Safety Car?
Simulates the race volatility if all SC and VSC laps were removed entirely. Orange line = what the chaos curve would look like with pure racing laps only. Faint yellow verticals mark the removed neutralisation laps.
0.7212
Actual Volatility
full race, all laps
0.7304
No-SC Volatility
74 racing laps only
+1.3%
Difference
more chaotic without SC
4
SC/VSC Laps
laps removed from calc
Faint yellow lines mark removed SC/VSC laps · Orange = what the race chaos would have looked like
Momentum Tracker
Rolling 5-lap position change per driver. Positive = charging forward, negative = falling back. Shows who had the most momentum at each phase of the race. Defaults to top 10 finishers — use filters to explore all drivers.
Rolling 5-lap position change per driver. Positive = gaining, Negative = losing positions.
HUL-7
peak at L12
ANT-6
peak at L69
TSU-5
peak at L74
COL-4
peak at L13
STR-4
peak at L18
Quick select:10 / 20 drivers shown
Strategy Chaos Score
Measures how much strategy decisions shaped the race outcome. Counts undercuts, overcuts, pit-cycle passes, and failed strategies.
197
Strategy Chaos Score
Undercuts
11
Pitted early, gained on exit
Overcuts
62
Stayed out, gained via track position
Pit-Cycle Passes
73
Positions gained through pit windows
Failed Strategies
18
Net position loss from pit strategy

F1 Chaos Analyzer is an independent, unofficial fan project and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to Formula 1, Formula One Management Ltd., the FIA, or any F1 team or driver. "Formula 1", "F1", and related marks are trademarks of Formula One Licensing BV. Race data sourced via the FastF1 library for non-commercial, educational purposes.
